rows.src: No published GI; sea vegetables have negligible glycemic impact at typical servings. USDA FoodData for carb data.

rows.name: Broccoli sprouts
rows.src: Sugarfit glycemic index of sprouts reference; broccoli sprouts GI 15

rows.src: Blue Circle Diabetes Foundation; mung sprouts GI 32, GL 1.5

rows.name: Cluster beans
rows.src: Estimated from high-fiber legume vegetable; guar gum content slows glucose absorption. Freedom from Diabetes confirms cluster beans as anti-diabetic.

rows.name: Tindora
rows.src: Estimated from gourd family GI; Instagram reference from diabetes expert confirms ivy gourd is low GI, insulin-mimicking compounds.

rows.name: Lotus stem
rows.src: Logi Food Coach database; lotus root GI 33 (raw). Boiled form estimated slightly higher at 38 based on cooking starch changes.

rows.name: Maitake mushroom
rows.aliases: maitake|hen of the woods|sheep's head mushroom|dancing mushroom
rows.src: Estimated from mushroom family GI; all mushroom varieties GI 10-30. USDA FoodData for carb data.

rows.name: Palm heart
rows.src: Estimated from palm pulp GI 20 (glycemic-index.net); palm heart is similar in profile with very low carb content.

rows.name: Physalis
rows.src: Glycemic index and glycemic load of fruits complete chart — glycemic-index.net; physalis GI 15

rows.name: Jerusalem artichoke
rows.src: Glycemic index and glycemic load of vegetables — glycemic-index.net; Jerusalem artichoke (fresh) GI 50

rows.src: Glycaemic index and glycaemic load of commonly consumed Thai fruits IFRJ 2021; sala classified low GI

rows.name: Prickly pear
rows.src: Glycemic index and glycemic load of fruits complete chart — glycemic-index.net; prickly pear GI 35

rows.name: Acerola
rows.src: Glycemic index and glycemic load of fruits complete chart — glycemic-index.net; acerola GI 20

rows.name: Kalanamak rice
rows.aliases: kala namak|black salt rice|UP aromatic rice
rows.src: Estimated low-GI: certified GI ≤55 by producers; amylose ~25%; analogous to Basmati (GI 52). Source: Amalfarm/producers citing GI 49–52. USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168878) for carb values.

rows.name: Gobindobhog rice
rows.src: Estimated high GI: short-grain high-amylopectin variety; nutritionist commentary indicates GI higher than basmati, comparable to standard white rice. Grainka.com; USDA FoodData for carbs.

rows.name: Ambemohar rice
rows.aliases: ambe mohar|mango blossom rice|Maharashtra fragrant rice
rows.src: Blue Circle Diabetes Foundation: GI=73, GL=22 per 100g for boiled Ambemohar rice. https://www.bluecircle.foundation/food/boiled-ambe-mohar-rice

rows.name: Seeraga samba rice
rows.src: Estimated low GI based on producer and dietitian consensus: lower GI vs white rice, high fiber content, traditional Tamil Nadu variety with GI tag. Slurrp.com; USDA analogues for carbs.

rows.name: IR64 rice
rows.src: Science.gov / NIN study: IR64 has GI and GL similar to Jyothi (white rice standard); amylose ~22–25%. science.gov topicpages rice variety IR64.

rows.name: Navara rice
rows.src: Science.gov (NIN/ICMR study): Njavara (Navara) GI and GL similar to Jyothi and IR64; despite medicinal status. science.gov rice variety IR64 topic pages 2018.

rows.name: Pokkali rice
rows.form: Pokkali rice, boiled (salt-tolerant Kerala heritage rice)
rows.src: OGTT human trials (Instagram reel citing published study): GI=41.7, lowest among major South Indian varieties. Instagram @pokkali posting study result, 2025.

rows.name: Indrayani rice
rows.form: Indrayani rice, boiled (short grain aromatic, Maharashtra)
rows.src: Blue Circle Diabetes Foundation: GI=73, GL=29 per 100g for Indrayani rice. https://www.bluecircle.foundation/food/indrayani-rice

rows.name: Bamboo rice
rows.form: Bamboo rice, boiled (seeds of flowering bamboo)
rows.src: Heritage-bowls.com / Netmeds: GI=20, lowest among rice types; high fiber content. Estimated — no clinical trial; based on producer analysis of starch digestibility.

rows.name: Swarna rice (MTU 7029)
rows.src: Estimated from white rice analogue: high-amylopectin, low-amylose variety; analogous to generic white rice GI=73. International Tables 2021 context; USDA for carbs.

rows.name: Japanese Koshihikari rice
rows.form: Koshihikari rice, boiled (short-grain Japanese premium variety)
rows.src: PMC8701134 (Foods journal 2021): Koshihikari predicted highest glycemic impact among non-glutinous Japanese cultivars; high amylopectin content. J Oleo Sci 2024 Kawada et al confirms postprandial glucose response.

rows.name: Riceberry rice
rows.form: Riceberry rice, boiled (Thai antioxidant-rich dark variety)
rows.src: Multiple sources: medium-to-low GI classification; riceberry has high anthocyanin and fiber content lowering glycemic response compared to white rice. Thenaturalhealthmarket.co.uk 2016; Timeout Bangkok 2019.

rows.name: Arborio rice
rows.src: Foodstruct.com: GI=69 (medium) for arborio rice. PMC8701134 confirms Carnaroli (close relative) high amylopectin therefore higher glycemic impact.

rows.name: Wild rice (Zizania)
rows.aliases: wild rice|Zizania aquatica|Canadian wild rice|black wild rice
rows.form: Wild rice, boiled (Zizania aquatica, North American)
rows.src: PubMed 25997214 (Zhang et al 2015): GI of wild rice=53.72 in in vivo study; low GI food. Foodstruct confirms GI=57. Mendosa tables GI=45.

rows.name: Calrose rice
rows.aliases: calrose|US medium grain rice|California sushi rice
rows.src: PMC8701134 (Foods 2021): Calrose ranked third-highest glycemic impact among cultivars studied due to high amylopectin content. Mendosa tables (2008) also cite Calrose rice bread GI=72.

rows.name: Instant rice (precooked)
rows.form: Instant rice, rehydrated (precooked dehydrated white rice)
rows.src: International Tables 2002/2008 (Foster-Powell); glycemic-index.net GI=85 for instant rice. PubMed 30536382 confirms high GI due to pregelatinized starch. USDA FDC cooked instant rice ~28g carbs/100g.

rows.name: French Camargue red rice
rows.aliases: Camargue red rice|French red rice|riz rouge de Camargue
rows.form: Camargue red rice, boiled (French nutty whole grain)
rows.src: Estimated from red rice analogue: Camargue red rice is whole grain with high fiber; red rice GI reported at 55 (Diabesmart.in, Vively.com.au). Fiber content from product labels.

rows.name: Rice bran
rows.form: Rice bran, raw (outer layer of rice grain)
rows.src: Sydney GI Database / Foster-Powell 2002: rice bran GI=19 (very low, high insoluble fiber). USDA FDC ID 168878 for carb/fiber values.

rows.name: Adrak chai (ginger tea)
rows.form: Adrak chai, with milk and sugar (ginger spiced tea)
rows.form_hi: Adrak wali chai, doodh aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated from standard masala chai analogue (GI ~55) with sugar (sucrose GI=65); ginger has negligible carbs and may lower GI slightly. Carbs based on ~10g sugar per 150ml serving.

rows.name: Kashmiri kahwa
rows.form: Kashmiri kahwa, with honey and saffron (spiced green tea)
rows.form_hi: Kahwa, shahad aur kesar ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: green tea base (GI negligible) + honey ~8g per serving (honey GI=61); no direct study. Carbs from standard kahwa recipe analysis.

rows.name: Filter coffee (South Indian)
rows.aliases: filter kaapi|South Indian coffee|degree coffee|decoction coffee
rows.form: Filter coffee, with full-fat milk and sugar (South Indian style)
rows.form_hi: Filter kaapi, doodh aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: coffee base has negligible GI; sugar (sucrose GI=65) ~8–10g per 150ml serving; full-fat milk GI ~27. Composite estimate ~52. Impulse Coffees; Ultrahuman CGM blog.

rows.name: Haldi doodh (golden milk)
rows.form: Haldi doodh, with full-fat milk and sugar (turmeric milk)
rows.src: Estimated: full-fat milk base GI=39; turmeric has negligible carbs, curcumin may reduce postprandial glucose; ~10g sugar per 200ml. Composite low GI estimated ~34.

rows.name: Green tea (plain)
rows.src: International Tables 2021: plain unsweetened green tea — negligible carbs, GI not applicable. USDA FDC ID 171917.

rows.name: Sulaimani chai
rows.aliases: sulaimani tea|black tea lemon|Kerala black tea|Malabar tea
rows.form: Sulaimani chai, with lemon and sugar (South Indian black spiced tea)
rows.form_hi: Sulaimani chai, nimbu aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: black tea base negligible GI; ~8g sugar (sucrose GI=65) per 150ml; lemon negligible. GL estimated from sucrose content.

rows.name: Jigarthanda
rows.aliases: jil jil jigarthanda|Madurai cold drink|almond gum milk drink
rows.form: Jigarthanda, traditional Madurai style (milk, almond gum, nannari, ice cream)
rows.src: Bon Happetee / SnapCalorie: Estimated GI=48 (Bon Happetee), carbs ~50g per 300ml serving. Milk base GI ~35; added sugar/ice cream raises GI moderately.

rows.name: Rose lassi
rows.form: Rose lassi, with rose syrup and yogurt (sweet)
rows.form_hi: Gulab lassi, gulab sharbat aur dahi ke sath
rows.src: Estimated from sweet lassi analogue (GI ~52): yogurt base GI~36 + rose syrup sucrose GI=65; composite weighted estimate. Carbs from standard recipe: ~30g sugar/yogurt per 250ml.

rows.name: Banana lassi
rows.form: Banana lassi, with banana, yogurt and sugar
rows.form_hi: Kele ki lassi, dahi aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: yogurt GI~36 + banana GI~51 + sugar; composite estimate ~54. Carbs: ~35g per 250ml from banana (~12g) and yogurt sugars (~23g with added sugar).

rows.name: Cold coffee (with milk and sugar)
rows.form: Cold coffee, with full-fat milk and sugar (Indian style, no ice cream)
rows.form_hi: Thanda coffee, doodh aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: coffee negligible GI; milk GI=39; ~15g sugar (sucrose GI=65) per 250ml; composite weighted ~41.

rows.name: Jaljeera
rows.form: Jaljeera, with black salt, cumin, lemon and sugar (summer cooler)
rows.form_hi: Jaljeera, kala namak, jeera, nimbu aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: primarily water + ~8g sugar (sucrose GI=65) per 200ml; cumin water itself has near-zero GI. Composite estimated ~63.

rows.name: Panakam
rows.aliases: panagam|panaka|jaggery lemon drink|temple drink Tamil Nadu
rows.form: Panakam, with jaggery, lemon and ginger (South Indian festival drink)
rows.form_hi: Panakam, gur, nimbu aur adrak ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: jaggery GI=84 (International Tables context); ~20g jaggery per 200ml serving; composite GI ~84. USDA for jaggery carbs.

rows.name: Kokum sharbat
rows.form: Kokum sharbat, with water and sugar (sweet-sour cooler)
rows.form_hi: Kokum sharbat, paani aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: kokum extract is acidic/hydroxycitric acid which may lower GI; ~12g sugar per 200ml. Composite low-med GI ~55. No direct study found.

rows.name: Sol kadhi
rows.form: Sol kadhi, with kokum and coconut milk (Maharashtra digestive beverage)
rows.form_hi: Sol kadhi, nariyal doodh aur kokum ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: coconut milk (low GI ~40) + kokum (acidic, no sugar by default); no added sugar version; composite very low GI ~25. No direct GI study.

rows.name: Pomegranate juice
rows.form: Pomegranate juice, fresh pressed, no sugar
rows.form_hi: Anar ka juice, taaza, bina cheeni
rows.src: Sydney GI Database / multiple studies: pomegranate juice GI=53 (low). USDA FDC ID 167762 for carb values: ~15g carb/100ml.

rows.name: Watermelon juice
rows.form: Watermelon juice, fresh pressed, no sugar
rows.form_hi: Tarbooz ka juice, taaza, bina cheeni
rows.src: International Tables 2021 / Mendosa tables: watermelon GI=76 (high), but GL is moderate due to high water content. USDA FDC ID 167765.

rows.name: Neera (palm sap)
rows.form: Neera, fresh unfermented coconut palm sap
rows.src: Scribd Jurnal Nira / Trinidad et al 2010: Neera/coconut sap GI=35 (low). Drlogy.com cites coconut sap GI=35 vs table sugar GI=70.

rows.name: Ragi malt drink
rows.aliases: ragi porridge drink|ragi satva|finger millet drink|koozh
rows.form: Ragi malt, with water and sugar (finger millet fermented drink)
rows.form_hi: Ragi malt, paani aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Ragi GI=54–68 (Sugarfit, AskNestle: GI=68). Fermented ragi drink slightly lower GI than cooked. Sugar added ~15g. Composite estimated ~68.

rows.name: Jeera water
rows.form: Jeera water, plain, no sugar (boiled cumin water)
rows.src: Negligible carbohydrate content — cumin seeds steeped in water contribute minimal carbs. GI not applicable. Indian Express / Indian Ayurveda sources.

rows.name: ABC juice
rows.aliases: apple beet carrot juice|ABC drink|detox juice
rows.form: ABC juice, fresh pressed (apple, beetroot, carrot blend)
rows.form_hi: ABC juice, seb, chukandar aur gajar
rows.src: Estimated composite: apple juice GI=41 (Int. Tables 2021), beetroot juice GI~64, carrot juice GI~43; blend average ~43. Carbs from USDA component values.

rows.name: Wheatgrass juice
rows.src: Estimated: very low carb content; a 2016 rat study (Medical News Today) showed wheatgrass raised insulin and lowered blood glucose; GI estimated very low ~15 from negligible carbs.

rows.name: Frooti (mango drink)
rows.form: Frooti, packaged mango drink (Parle, ~11.5% mango pulp)
rows.form_hi: Frooti, pakka hua aam ka drinks
rows.src: Eatthismuch: 160ml Frooti = 26g carbs (25g sugar). Sucrose GI=65; fructose-glucose mix estimated at ~67. LinkedIn/Food Pharmer: ~10.5 tsp sugar per 300ml.

rows.name: Bournvita (malt drink with milk)
rows.form: Bournvita, with full-fat milk and hot water (standard preparation)
rows.src: Estimated: Bournvita = malt extract (high GI ~80) + milk (GI~39) + sugar; Ultrahuman CGM data shows high glucose spike in 69% of users; composite GI ~65. CGM data from blog.ultrahuman.com.

rows.name: Red Bull energy drink
rows.form: Red Bull energy drink, standard (250ml can)
rows.src: Bon Happetee (estimated GI=58); Glycemic-Index.net estimates low-medium. Carbs: standard Red Bull 250ml ~27.5g sugar/carb.

rows.name: Kombucha (plain)
rows.src: PMC9982099 (Frontiers Nutrition 2023): kombucha reduced meal GI from 86 to 68; standalone kombucha GI estimated ~40 (low residual sugar, acetic acid slows glucose absorption). DOI: 10.3389/fnut.2023.1039714

rows.name: Sesame seeds (white)
rows.src: Estimated low GI: very high fat, protein and fiber; negligible net carbs. Glycemic-index.net: tahini (sesame paste) GI=40 as analogue. USDA FDC (sesame seeds USDA 12023): carbs 11.73g, fiber 11.6g per 100g.

rows.name: Sunflower seeds
rows.src: Estimated low GI from high fat/protein composition. Net carbs ~11.4g/100g. Healthline seeds review. USDA FDC ID 12036: carbs 20g, fiber 8.6g per 100g.

rows.name: Pumpkin seeds
rows.src: Estimated low GI: high fat and protein; low net carbs ~16.9g/100g. Klinio/diabetes sources confirm very low glycemic response. USDA FDC pumpkin seeds: carbs ~18g, fiber ~1.1g per 100g.

rows.name: Hemp seeds
rows.src: Klinio diabetes database: hemp seeds GI=15. Indian Express (Feb 2024): low GI, 31.56g carbs per 100g raw hemp seeds. USDA FDC ID 170148.

rows.name: Sabja seeds (basil seeds)
rows.form: Sabja seeds, soaked in water (Ocimum basilicum seeds)
rows.src: Estimated very low GI: gel-forming fiber (mucilage) dramatically slows glucose absorption. Similar to chia seeds (GI~1). Net carbs ~19.5g/100g. Commonly cited as diabetes-friendly. No direct study found.

rows.name: Poppy seeds (khus khus)
rows.src: Estimated low GI: high fat (46g/100g) and very high fiber; net carbs ~8.6g/100g. No direct GI study. USDA FDC ID 12157 for carb/fiber values.

rows.name: Watermelon seeds
rows.src: Estimated very low GI: high protein (28g/100g) and fat (47g/100g) composition; low net carbs. No direct GI study. USDA FDC for approximate values.

rows.name: Nigella seeds (kalonji)
rows.aliases: kalonji|nigella sativa|black cumin seeds|onion seeds|black seed
rows.form: Nigella seeds, raw (Nigella sativa, black cumin)
rows.src: Estimated very low GI: N. sativa has well-documented blood glucose-lowering properties (multiple studies). High fiber, net carbs ~22.5g/100g. No direct GI test found.

rows.name: Teff grain
rows.src: PMC9416382 (Diabetes Metab Syndr Obes 2022): teff has low-to-moderate GI; Dereje et al and Assefa et al avg GI=36.7 for teff injera. Healthline: cooked whole teff GI=57. USDA FDC cooked teff: ~24g carbs/100g cooked.

rows.name: Fonio
rows.src: Terra Ingredients / WebMD: fonio GI ranges 35–49 (low GI food); low GI due to protein and fiber content. No ISO-compliant human trial found; estimate from multiple secondary sources.

rows.name: Goji berries (dried)
rows.src: Logi Food Coach / Glycemic-Index.net: goji berries GI=25–29 (low). USDA FoodData: goji berries dried 77.1g carb, 13g fiber per 100g (FDC).

rows.name: Acai berry (dried/powder)
rows.aliases: acai berries|acai powder|Amazon palm berry|euterpe oleracea
rows.src: Estimated very low GI: extremely high fiber (14.2g/100g) and fat (32g/100g); net carbs only ~9.8g/100g. No direct human GI study found. Widely described as low GI superfood.

rows.name: Sea buckthorn berries
rows.src: Estimated low GI: very tart, high acid content; low sugar, high fiber. Net carbs ~5.4g/100g. No direct GI study. USDA FDC approximations used.

rows.name: Spirulina powder
rows.src: USDA FDC ID 170495: spirulina dried 2.4g carbs, 0.4g fiber per 100g. Negligible carb content — GI not applicable at typical serving. PMC10494644 / PMC9787962 confirm no glycemic impact as standalone.

rows.name: Chlorella powder
rows.src: Estimated: at typical 10g serving, chlorella delivers only ~1.7g carbs — GI not applicable. Generally described as negligible glycemic impact. No direct clinical GI study found.

rows.name: Maca root powder
rows.form: Maca root powder, dried (gelatinized or raw)
rows.src: Estimated: maca 78g carbs/100g (themacateam.com), 9.8% fiber. Glucosinolates in maca may improve insulin sensitivity (PMC8194906 2021). No direct GI study; estimated ~55 from high-fiber starchy root analogue.

rows.name: Ashwagandha root powder
rows.src: At typical 5g serving, only ~2.8g carbs (mostly fiber) — GI not applicable. High withanolide content may improve insulin sensitivity. No direct GI study. WOWMD/Healthline for general composition.

rows.name: Moringa leaf powder
rows.aliases: moringa powder|drumstick leaf powder|moringa oleifera|shevaga paan churna
rows.src: Logi Food Coach: moringa leaf powder GI=25 (low). Clearcals: GI=55 as per 5g serving. Using Logi's more widely cited value of 25. USDA/Clearcals for carb values: 41g carbs, 36.8g fiber per 100g.

rows.name: Macadamia nuts
rows.src: Nuts for Life (nutsforlife.com.au): macadamia GI=NA (insufficient carbs to test). Verywell Health confirms low carb, glycemic control via carb displacement. USDA FDC ID 12131.

rows.name: Brazil nut
rows.src: Nuts for Life: Brazil nut GI=NA (insufficient carbs; only 2.4g carbs/100g). Verywell Health: selenium-rich, may improve insulin sensitivity. USDA FDC ID 12078.

rows.name: Pine nuts
rows.form: Pine nuts, raw (Pinus pinea or P. gerardiana for India)
rows.src: Nuts for Life: pine nut GI=NA (carbs 4.5g/100g, insufficient for testing). USDA FDC ID 12147.

rows.name: Chestnut
rows.src: Nuts for Life (citing International Tables): chestnut GI=54 (only nut with enough carbs; low GI). USDA FDC ID 12098.

rows.name: Tahini (sesame paste)
rows.form: Tahini, sesame seed paste (ground sesame seeds)
rows.src: Glycemic-Index.net: sesame paste/tahini GI=40. Blue Circle Diabetes Foundation: tahini GI=Low. USDA FDC ID 12155 for carbs.

rows.name: Almond butter
rows.form: Almond butter, plain, no added sugar
rows.src: Foodstruct.com: almond butter GI not formally tested; estimated low GI ~25 due to high fiber (10.3g) and fat (55.5g) per 100g. Peanut butter analogue GI=14. USDA FDC for carb values.

rows.name: Cacao nibs
rows.aliases: cacao nibs|raw cacao|cocoa nibs|cacao beans crushed
rows.form: Cacao nibs, raw (unsweetened crushed cacao beans)
rows.src: Whitakers Chocolates / Fully Healthy: cacao nibs low GI; 10g carbs with 5g fiber per 30g serving. Net carbs ~1g/100g. Widely described as low/negligible GI. USDA FDC approximate values.

rows.name: Dark chocolate (70%)
rows.src: Mendosa tables 2008 / Krack'd Snacks: dark chocolate 70% GI=23. Multiple sources confirm GI 23–25. USDA FDC ID 170273.

rows.name: Dark chocolate (85%+)
rows.aliases: dark chocolate 85%|extra dark chocolate|90% cacao chocolate
rows.src: Krack'd Snacks / Reddit Paleo analysis: 85%+ chocolate even lower GI than 70%; estimated ~20. Net carbs ~16.5g/100g. USDA FDC ID 170274 for carbs.

rows.name: Erythritol
rows.src: Multiple authoritative sources: erythritol GI=0 (not metabolized); does not affect blood glucose or insulin. NY Nutrition Group; Nutrisense.io; ISO-compliant tests.

rows.name: Yacon syrup
rows.form: Yacon syrup, liquid (from Smallanthus sonchifolius root)
rows.src: Darwin-Nutrition.fr: yacon syrup GI=10–30. Herbs-America.com: low GI due to fructooligosaccharides (FOS) content. Average ~20 used.

rows.name: Stevia
rows.form: Stevia extract, powder (steviol glycosides, zero calorie)
rows.src: International Tables 2021 context: steviol glycosides do not raise blood glucose — GI=0. ISO-compliant evidence. FDA GRAS status confirming zero metabolizable carbs.

rows.name: Nutritional yeast
rows.src: Glycemic-Index.net: yeast GI=35 (low). WebMD: nutritional yeast is low-glycemic food; beta-glucan fiber aids blood sugar regulation. Per 15g: ~4.5g carbs (WebMD per 2tbsp = 3g carbs).

rows.name: Apple cider vinegar
rows.form: Apple cider vinegar, unfiltered, with mother
rows.src: PubMed 31451249 (Khezri et al 2018 clinical trial): ACV improves glycemic indices; standalone ACV negligible carbs — GI not applicable. Acetic acid lowers postprandial glycemia of co-consumed foods.

rows.name: Pea protein powder
rows.aliases: pea protein|yellow pea protein|plant protein powder
rows.form: Pea protein isolate, powder (yellow split pea)
rows.src: PMC12857942 (PLOS One 2026): pea protein significantly reduces glycemic response when co-consumed with glucose; standalone GI negligible (protein isolate, ~5g carbs/100g). 33Fuel: low GI.

rows.name: Pineapple juice (fresh)
rows.form: Pineapple juice, fresh pressed, no sugar
rows.form_hi: Ananas ka juice, taaza, bina cheeni
rows.src: Mendosa tables / International Tables 2021: pineapple GI=59 (medium). Pineapple juice similar to fresh pineapple. USDA FDC ID 167753.

rows.name: Nannari sharbat
rows.form: Nannari sharbat, with water and sugar (Indian sarsaparilla cooler)
rows.form_hi: Nannari sharbat, paani aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: primarily sugar syrup (sucrose GI=65) diluted in water; nannari root has negligible carbs. ~24g sugar per 200ml. Composite estimated ~65.

rows.name: Falooda drink
rows.form: Falooda, with rose syrup, sabja, vermicelli, and milk (dessert drink)
rows.form_hi: Falooda, gulab sharbat, sabja, seviyan aur doodh ke sath
rows.src: Estimated composite: milk GI~39, rose syrup GI~65, vermicelli GI~58, sabja seeds very low; ~60g carbs per 300ml serving. Composite GI ~58.

rows.name: Irani chai
rows.aliases: Irani tea|Hyderabad Irani chai|dum tea Irani
rows.form: Irani chai, with evaporated milk (mawa) and sugar
rows.form_hi: Irani chai, khoya doodh aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: black tea base + condensed/evaporated milk (higher sugar density than regular milk) + sugar; ~15g sugar per 150ml. Composite GI ~55.

rows.name: Kanji (fermented carrot drink)
rows.aliases: kanji|black carrot kanji|gajar kanji|mustard carrot fermented drink
rows.form: Kanji, fermented (black carrot, mustard seeds, water — Holi drink)
rows.form_hi: Kanji, kali gajar, rai aur paani se fermented
rows.src: Estimated: lacto-fermented drink; acetic acid and lactic acid lower glycemic response. Carrot GI~39 (boiled); fermentation lowers further. ~12–15g carbs per 250ml from black carrot. Estimated ~35.

rows.name: Khus sharbat (vetiver cooler)
rows.form: Khus sharbat, with water and sugar (vetiver grass cooler)
rows.form_hi: Khus sharbat, paani aur cheeni ke sath
rows.src: Estimated: khus syrup (sugar base GI=65) diluted in water; ~20g sugar per 200ml. Vetiver root infusion itself has negligible carbs. Composite ~65.

rows.name: Korean short grain rice
rows.form: Korean short grain rice, boiled (standard Sindongjin variety)
rows.src: PMC12738849 (Frontiers Plant Science 2025): Sindongjin commercial Korean rice GI=76.85±2.82 in vitro. Multiple Korean rice varieties tested with GI ranging 52–88.

rows.name: Mango juice (fresh)
rows.aliases: mango juice|aam ras drink|fresh mango juice|aam ka ras
rows.form: Mango juice, fresh pressed, no added sugar
rows.form_hi: Aam ka juice, taaza, bina cheeni
rows.src: International Tables 2021 / Mendosa: mango GI=51 (low-medium). Juice slightly higher than whole fruit. USDA FDC ID 167755.
